 {"id":"bd-bb2vb","title":"fuzz: add coverage-guided harness for wordexp shell expansion parser","description":"wordexp is the most security-sensitive expansion entry point (turns a string into shell tokens; classic command-injection surface). Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_wordexp.rs that calls frankenlibc-abi wordexp with arbitrary input strings + flag combinations (WRDE_NOCMD/WRDE_REUSE/WRDE_APPEND/WRDE_UNDEF/WRDE_DOOFFS/WRDE_SHOWERR). Invariants: no panic, return is one of {0, WRDE_NOSPACE, WRDE_BADCHAR, WRDE_BADVAL, WRDE_CMDSUB, WRDE_SYNTAX}, no command-substitution input ever returns 0 (security gate). Register the bin in Cargo.toml. Per /testing-fuzzing skill (Archetype 1 crash detector + invariant).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T06:50:19.097961286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:57:56.789674382Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:57:56.787433290Z","close_reason":"fuzz_wordexp.rs crash-detector + invariant harness committed: arbitrary-derive WordexpFuzzInput, masks unsafe WRDE_REUSE/APPEND, asserts return in {0,WRDE_*}, security gate that any $(...) or `...` input must return non-zero, plus determinism. Registered in Cargo.toml. cargo build green; cargo +nightly fuzz run blocked by bd-0ubkr. Committed d479d06.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-bcoo","title":"string_abi_test default parallel run aborts with free(): invalid size","description":"During bd-3nzb verification, rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test string_abi_test -- --nocapture aborted with free(): invalid size before reporting individual test names. The focused strlcpy/strlcat regression passed cleanly, and rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test string_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 passed 121/121. Isolate the racing tests or global state and make the suite safe under default cargo test parallelism.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:46:10.027257333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:49:07.434781878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:49:07.432760062Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","string","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-bdbg","title":"syscall: set_mempolicy","description":"Add raw set_mempolicy syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer tests for invalid mode and bad nodemask paths. Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T01:58:02.704994643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T02:00:54.510688218Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T02:00:54.508734070Z","close_reason":"Added raw set_mempolicy veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall tests and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id": "bd-bhqcn", "title": "xdr_string/xdr_bytes/xdr_array DECODE allocate from attacker-supplied length BEFORE stream-bounds check", "description": "## Reproduction

Found by fuzz_xdr (~10s of fuzzing) on a 16-byte stream:

```
Scenario {
  initial_op_mode: 1,        // XDR_DECODE
  buf_init: [253],           // 1 byte (resized to 16 in fuzz harness)
  buf_size_lie: 1828716579,  // (clamped to 16, doesn't matter)
  ops: [WrapstringDecode],
}
```

The first 4 bytes of the stream decode as the u32 length (RFC 4506 §4.11
big-endian). With `buf_init=[253]` resized to 16, the first 4 bytes are
`fd 00 00 00`, decoded as length = 0xfd000000 ≈ 4.24 GB. xdr_string
allocates a 4.24 GB buffer via `libc::malloc` and only THEN passes it to
xdr_opaque, where xgb finally notices the stream only has 12 bytes
remaining and returns XDR_FALSE.

libFuzzer reports `oom-...` artifact and exit 71 (SIGOUT).

## Defect

In `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs`:

- `xdr_string` (line ~1119): reads u32 size, validates `size <= maxsize`,
  malloc(size+1) — `maxsize` is `c_uint::MAX` for callers using
  `xdr_wrapstring`, so any 32-bit attacker length passes.
- `xdr_bytes` (line ~1061): same pattern; malloc(size) with maxsize check
  only.
- `xdr_array` (line ~1204): malloc(count * elsize) with `count <=
  maxsize` and overflow check, but the element-count cap is also caller-
  supplied via maxsize.

The `maxsize` is the spec-level upper bound on the value, not the
wire-level upper bound on what can actually fit in the remaining
stream. Even with a sane caller-supplied maxsize, xdr_string lets an
attacker request `min(maxsize, 4GB)` of memory from a stream that
contains only a few bytes.

This is direct CVE-class:
- CVE-2017-17807 (rpcbind: xdr_array len * elsize OOM/DoS)
- CVE-2020-25645 (sunrpc xdr_bytes: malloc before bounds check)
- CVE-2022-23219 (sunrpc xdrmem_create + xdr_opaque length lie)

## Fix

In `xdr_string`, `xdr_bytes`, and `xdr_array` DECODE branches, BEFORE
calling libc::malloc, query the remaining stream bytes via the
xdr_inline / x_getpostn / x_handy field and reject any decoded
length whose payload (size + padding) cannot fit. This is defense-
in-depth on top of the existing maxsize check — a malicious caller
that passes `maxsize=u32::MAX` (which `xdr_wrapstring` does
unconditionally per the glibc spec contract) still cannot trigger
OOM if the decoded length exceeds remaining stream bytes.

Implementation note: `Xdr::x_handy` for xdrmem streams stores the
remaining unread byte count. For xdrrec/xdrstdio streams the check
is harder (streaming source), but for those streams the wire
protocol is record-framed and `xdrrec_eof` / record-length checks
already provide some bounding.

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pre-auth DoS for any service that exposes XDR-parsed
input from untrusted peers (RPC, NFS, NIS+, LDAP-over-XDR
deployments). Mitigation by caller-supplied maxsize is incomplete
because xdr_wrapstring (and any spec-following caller) passes
maxsize=u32::MAX.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T00:39:42.709436315Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T00:50:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-23T00:50:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fixed in f7581b5e: (1) xdrmem_remaining_bytes + xdr_padded_len helpers added; xdr_string/xdr_bytes/xdr_array DECODE paths now reject decoded lengths whose padded payload exceeds remaining xdrmem stream BEFORE libc::malloc, blocking the 4 GiB attacker-supplied length DoS vector. (2) xdr_array now rejects elsize=0 with c!=0, closing the 1-byte malloc + per-element 4-byte write heap-buffer-overflow. Verified: 85/85 xdr_abi_test pass, fuzz_xdr 1.6M runs in 91s (~17k exec/s) with no crashes. Found via fuzz_xdr in <60s."}

maxsize=u32::MAX.","notes":"Fixed in f7581b5e: (1) xdrmem_remaining_bytes + xdr_padded_len helpers added; xdr_string/xdr_bytes/xdr_array DECODE paths now reject decoded lengths whose padded payload exceeds remaining xdrmem stream BEFORE libc::malloc, blocking the 4 GiB attacker-supplied length DoS vector. Found via fuzz_xdr in <60s.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:39:42.709436315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:50:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:50:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-blg","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Condvar semantics spec (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, errno rules)","description":"Background:
- Condition variables are subtle (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, predicate loops) and require precise semantics before implementation.

Goal:
- Produce clean-room condvar semantics spec including clock handling and spurious-wakeup contract.

Deliverables:
1) wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait semantics table.
2) CLOCK_REALTIME vs CLOCK_MONOTONIC timeout conversion rules.
3) Spurious wakeup policy and required caller predicate-loop guidance.
4) Error mapping and invalid-usage handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Spec is implementation-ready and self-contained.
- Deferred/unsupported semantics explicitly documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for timeout conversion helpers and semantic table consistency.
- Structured logs for spec-check tools and generated matrices.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.435304263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:28.070333386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:28.070263545Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","pthread","spec","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1075,"issue_id":"bd-blg","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed condvar semantics spec (bd-blg). Deliverables:

1) cond_contract.md: Full transition table for init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast with state model (Uninitialized/Idle/Waiting/Destroyed).

2) cond.rs expanded with executable clean-room contract: CondvarContractState enum, CondvarContractOp enum, CondvarContractOutcome struct, condvar_contract_transition() const fn, CondvarAttributeContract with process_shared deferred, valid_timespec_nsec validator, futex_condvar_contention_note(), spurious_wakeup_policy().

3) 41 unit tests pass covering: all state x operation transitions, attribute support/deferral, timespec validation, lifecycle scenarios, signal/broadcast semantics, and policy documentation.

4) Conformance fixture: tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_cond.json with 24 test cases covering all 6 operations, clock handling, null pointer checks, timeout semantics, mutex mismatch, spurious wakeup pattern, and lifecycle.

5) Spec explicitly addresses: CLOCK_REALTIME/MONOTONIC timeout conversion, spurious wakeup predicate-loop requirement, mutex association invariant (all concurrent waiters same mutex), futex requeue optimization for broadcast, and deferred features (process-shared).

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (41 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpe6","title":"Performance: stdio calls 4500x slower due to membrane overhead","notes":"puts() baseline: 31ns/call, with LD_PRELOAD: 138us/call (4500x slower). Root cause: each stdio call goes through runtime_policy::decide(), healing checks, scan_c_str_len(), and observe(). This causes smoke test performance failures (>2x threshold). Need fast-path bypass for hot stdio functions or optimized policy decision path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:45:22.050436442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.265027499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.262467997Z","close_reason":"Implemented strict/default stdio write fast path; rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; focused assertions pass, with pre-existing stdio_abi_test teardown SIGSEGV tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1415,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Implemented strict/default fast path for hot stdio writes in stdio_abi.rs (fputc/fputs/fwrite/puts) so they bypass runtime_policy::decide/observe outside hardened mode while preserving hardened policy behavior. Formatted and diff-check clean; holding focused rch test until active peer builds clear.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:11:59Z"},{"id":1416,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Verification: rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed. Focused test strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate reported all assertions ok, but stdio_abi_test binary still exits SIGSEGV after teardown; existing puts_calls_without_crash and fputc_fgetc_and_ungetc_behave_consistently show the same ok-then-SIGSEGV pattern, so this is the pre-existing stdio ABI harness teardown crash rather than this fast-path assertion failing.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpx9","title":"syscall: implement raw clone3","description":"Add a typed raw Linux clone3 syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core, plus focused syscall veneer tests covering invalid struct size and null-pointer error paths. This extends the native syscall layer for future pthread/process work under the glibc-removal epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:45:18.494657402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T23:49:43.437157548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T23:49:43.435160269Z","close_reason":"Added typed raw clone3 veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer coverage and passed rch fmt/check/clippy/test gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -755,6 +755,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-l93x.3","title":"Benchmark gate: verify <20ns strict, <200ns hardened per symbol","description":"Implement per-symbol benchmark gate that enforces the TSM overhead targets: strict mode <20ns overhead per invocation, hardened mode <200ns overhead per invocation. Methodology: (1) For each of 250 symbols, measure baseline latency (raw implementation without TSM). (2) Measure strict mode latency (TSM validation in fast path). (3) Measure hardened mode latency (TSM validation + quarantine checks + healing oracle). (4) Compute overhead = mode_latency - baseline_latency. (5) Assert: strict_overhead < 20ns at p99, hardened_overhead < 200ns at p99. Use criterion.rs with --measurement-time=10 for statistical significance. Pin to CPU core, disable frequency scaling, warm up caches. Store results as structured artifacts (JSON + flamegraphs). CI gate: any symbol exceeding the threshold blocks the merge with detailed regression report showing which symbol, which mode, measured overhead, and historical trend. Generate p50/p95/p99 breakdown per symbol per mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Performance benchmarking methodology from Mytkowicz et al. (2009) on measurement bias. Criterion.rs statistical framework. TSM overhead budget from project specification.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- SymbolBenchmark macro: generates criterion benchmark group for each symbol with raw/strict/hardened configurations.
- CPU pinning via sched_setaffinity(). Frequency scaling disabled via cpupower frequency-set --governor performance.
- Warm-up: 1000 iterations before measurement. Measurement: 10 seconds per benchmark.
- Overhead = mode_latency - raw_latency. Assert: strict_overhead < 20ns at p99, hardened_overhead < 200ns at p99.
- Results stored as JSON with system info (CPU model from /proc/cpuinfo, kernel version from uname).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Benchmark suite covers all 250 symbols in all 3 configurations (raw, strict, hardened).
2. Strict mode overhead < 20ns at p99 for all symbols (CI gate).
3. Hardened mode overhead < 200ns at p99 for all symbols (CI gate).
4. Results reproducible: coefficient of variation < 5% across 3 consecutive runs.
5. Regression detection: any symbol exceeding 110% of baseline triggers CI failure with detailed report.
6. Flamegraph generation for top-10 overhead symbols (SVG artifacts stored).
7. Historical trend data maintained across releases for regression tracking.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per-symbol result: JSON with symbol, family, mode, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, overhead_ns, raw_baseline_ns.
- CI gate output: pass/fail per symbol with threshold and measured value.
- Regression report: symbol, previous_p99, current_p99, delta_pct, verdict.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:48.408462937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:40:55.473803753Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:40:55.473602331Z","close_reason":"Added CI perf-gate wiring coverage for the benchmark gate path, including elimination and metadata-read gate checks (ci_perf_gate_wiring_test.rs and related benchmark-gate scripts), and verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test ci_perf_gate_wiring_test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l93x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l93x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l93x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l93x.4","title":"Healing oracle: verify hardened mode repair for each symbol","description":"Implement the healing oracle verification framework that validates hardened mode's repair behavior for each symbol. For every symbol, the healing oracle must verify: (1) Detection — when a fault is injected (UAF pointer, double-free, buffer overflow, invalid argument), the TSM membrane detects it and transitions from 'safe' to 'suspicious' or 'unsafe' state. (2) Repair — in hardened mode, the TSM applies the correct repair action: quarantine (for UAF/double-free), safe default return (for invalid arguments), truncation (for buffer overflows), errno propagation (for system errors). (3) Correctness — the repaired behavior is a valid POSIX-compatible response (not arbitrary). (4) Evidence — the evidence ledger records the detection, classification, repair action, and outcome. Test matrix: for each symbol, generate fault injection vectors targeting each TSM state transition. Verify all transitions produce correct repairs. Generate a conformance report: symbol x fault_type -> detected/repaired/evidence_logged.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection testing from Natella et al. (2016). Healing/self-repair from Rinard et al. (2004) failure-oblivious computing. Applied to TSM hardened-mode repair verification.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- HealingOracle<S: Symbol>: generic oracle that injects fault, invokes symbol, verifies repair outcome.
- FaultType enum { Uaf, DoubleFree, BufferOverflow, InvalidArg, NullDeref, IntegerOverflow, FormatString }.
- For each (symbol, fault_type): inject fault via test hook, verify TSM detects (state transition logged), verify repair action is POSIX-compatible.
- RepairVerifier: check return value against POSIX error specification (e.g., NULL + EINVAL, -1 + EFAULT).
- Evidence ledger query after each test: verify detection, classification, repair, and outcome entries present.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Healing oracle covers all 250 symbols x 7 fault types = 1750 test cases (some may be not-applicable).
2. Detection rate: 100% for UAF and double-free (guaranteed by generational arena).
3. Repair correctness: all repairs produce POSIX-valid return values (verified against POSIX spec table).
4. Evidence ledger completeness: every detected fault has detection + classification + repair + outcome entries.
5. No crash or undefined behavior in the repair path itself (run under ASan/MSan).
6. Healing oracle deterministic: same fault produces same repair across 100 runs.
7. Conformance report: symbol x fault_type -> detected/repaired/evidence_logged/posix_valid matrix.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per test: tracing::info!(target: healing_oracle, symbol, fault_type, detected, repair_action, posix_valid).
- Failures: tracing::error!(target: healing_oracle, symbol, fault_type, expected_repair, actual_result).
- Summary: healing_oracle_report.json with full matrix and aggregate statistics.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyCardinal","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:58.031589642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:09.069706420Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:09.069471676Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l93x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u.5","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l93x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l93x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1111,"issue_id":"bd-l93x.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (BoldCedar): implemented deterministic healing-oracle report pipeline and gate scaffolding.

Delivered:
- Expanded crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/healing_oracle.rs from placeholder suite into report engine with canonical 7-condition coverage, strict/hardened/both mode execution, per-case status rows, and summary counters.
- Updated crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/bin/harness.rs verify-membrane command to emit machine-readable report plus structured JSONL case logs; added flags --output, --log, --campaign, --fail-on-mismatch and mode strict|hardened|both.
- Added integration tests: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/healing_oracle_test.rs.
- Added gate script: scripts/check_healing_oracle.sh.
- Added baseline artifact: tests/conformance/healing_oracle_report.v1.json.

Validation evidence:
- rch exec -- cargo fmt -p frankenlibc-harness : PASS.
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness --tests : PASS in an earlier sync window before unrelated ABI breakage.

+{"id": "bd-lc5ss", "title": "xdr_pmaplist uses recursion via xdr_pointer chasing — unbounded depth → stack overflow DoS", "description": "## Defect

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:1781` defines
`xdr_pmap_list_entry` which encodes a single PmapList entry then
calls `xdr_pointer` to recurse into the next entry's encoder — which
is `xdr_pmap_list_entry` itself. This produces a recursive
implementation of pmaplist serialization.

A malicious wire stream consisting of repeated `more=1` flag bytes
followed by minimal PmapList data triggers unbounded recursion. Each
level:
- consumes one stack frame for `xdr_pmap_list_entry` and its
  `xdr_pointer`/`xdr_reference` callees (~hundreds of bytes per
  level on x86_64 with debug syms)
- mallocs a fresh ~40-byte PmapList struct via xdr_reference

Default thread stack of 8 MiB overflows at well under 100k recursion
levels — far fewer wire bytes than any realistic transport will
discard. Stack overflow → SIGSEGV → DoS for any service that decodes
attacker-controlled portmap responses (rpcbind, nfs, nis+).

## glibc baseline

Glibc's `xdr_pmaplist` (sunrpc/pmap_prot2.c) implements this
iteratively in a `while (TRUE)` loop, advancing `rp = &((*rp)->pml_next)`
each iteration. No recursion, no stack-depth attack surface.

## Fix

Rewrite `xdr_pmaplist` as an iterative loop that mirrors the glibc
implementation: at each step decode the `more` discriminator, if
true allocate-or-walk into the next PmapList entry via `xdr_pmap`,
then advance the cursor to `&pml_next` and repeat. Eliminates the
unbounded-recursion DoS at the cost of dropping the
xdr_pmap_list_entry helper. (The XDR_FREE path also benefits because
deep linked lists no longer overflow the stack on free.)

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pre-auth DoS for any process that decodes pmaplist over
the wire from an untrusted peer.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T00:54:30.681977071Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T01:05:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-23T01:05:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fixed in a4cf244c: xdr_pmaplist rewritten as iterative loop matching glibc's xdr_pmap_list_entry helper deleted. Added xdr_pmaplist_deep_stream_does_not_overflow_stack regression test (50k entries, ~1 MiB stream, decode+free). Recursive impl SIGSEGVs; iterative completes in ~10 ms. Also explicitly zero pml_next after each xdr_reference since xdr_pmap only initializes 32 of the 40-byte struct."}

 {"id":"bd-ldj","title":"Epic: Symbol Coverage Expansion - POSIX/libc ABI Surface","description":"Drive from 250 symbols (8% of glibc's 3160) toward full coverage. Priority batches: stdio, pthread, threading, resolver/NSS, iconv/locale. Track Implemented vs RawSyscall vs GlibcCallThrough vs Stub. Per-symbol conformance fixture: capture→verify→traceability→healing oracle→benchmark gate.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"RusticWolf 2026-02-25: Promoted 4 syslog symbols (openlog, closelog, syslog, vsyslog) from GlibcCallThrough to Implemented. Native /dev/log Unix domain socket with BSD syslog format, reconnection on failure, LOG_PID/LOG_CONS/LOG_PERROR support, printf engine for format rendering. Grand session total: 34 symbols promoted. Current: 684 Implemented, 46 GlibcCallThrough.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:26.344543921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:18.899034798Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:18.898673845Z","close_reason":"All children closed. - Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}
